channel.title: Linear A
channel.description: History wrote two scripts. Only one was ever deciphered. Fifteen-minute alt-history short fiction â€” a single household, a single document, a single journey, set in a world where one historical contingency went the other way. New episodes appear when they appear.
channel.itunes:author: Linear A
channel.itunes:summary: History wrote two scripts. Only one was ever deciphered. Fifteen-minute alt-history short fiction â€” a single household, a single document, a single journey, set in a world where one historical contingency went the other way. New episodes appear when they appear.

item.title: The Outrigger at Limuw
item.description: Polynesian Pacific / Channel Islands, 1300 CE (POD c. 1100 CE). pavane-elegiac at single-canoe scale.
item.itunes:summary: Polynesian Pacific / Channel Islands, 1300 CE (POD c. 1100 CE). pavane-elegiac at single-canoe scale.
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Bayt al-Hikma Auction
item.description: 848 CE. Caliph al-Mutawakkil dies of a fever before he reverses his predecessor al-Wathiq's policy. The Mu'tazilite *mihna* winds down quietly rather than being replaced by the Hanbali traditionalist current. The Caliph's young son is cautious and dependent on the Mu'tazilite-trained chief judges...
item.itunes:summary: 848 CE. Caliph al-Mutawakkil dies of a fever before he reverses his predecessor al-Wathiq's policy. The Mu'tazilite *mihna* winds down quietly rather than being replaced by the Hanbali traditionalist current. The Caliph's young son is cautious and dependent on the Mu'tazilite-trained chief judges...
item.itunes:author: Linear A

item.title: The Midwife of Kaskaskia
item.description: 1175 CE. The Moorehead-phase reorganisation of the Mississippian centre now called Cahokia succeeds where in our timeline it failed. After the great flooding cycle of c. 1150, a new political elite redistributes labour away from intensive ridged-field maize toward a mixed riverine economy — the *...
item.itunes:summary: 1175 CE. The Moorehead-phase reorganisation of the Mississippian centre now called Cahokia succeeds where in our timeline it failed. After the great flooding cycle of c. 1150, a new political elite redistributes labour away from intensive ridged-field maize toward a mixed riverine economy — the *...
item.itunes:author: Linear A
